question_structure.context: A transformer with two secondary coils has a primary coil with $n_{1} = 100$ turns, connected to a circuit with a voltage of $U_{1} = 220\mathrm{V}$.
question_structure.sub_question_1: How can the number of turns \(n_{2}\) and \(n_{3}\) be determined to obtain voltages \(U_{2} = 6\mathrm{V}\) and \(U_{3} = 110\mathrm{V}\) on the two secondary coils, respectively?
question_structure.sub_question_2: If two appliances, "6 V 20 W" and "110 V 60 W," are connected to the two secondary coils respectively, what is the input current in the primary coil?

sub_question_1.step_1: According to the relationship between the voltage and the number of turns in the primary and secondary coils, from $ n_{2} \times 100 = 30 $

sub_question_2.step_3: Let the input current of the primary coil be \( I_{1} \), and from \( P_{\text{in}} = P_{\text{out}} \)

image_captions: The image shows a schematic diagram of an ideal transformer with one primary coil (n1) connected to an input voltage (U1) and two secondary coils (n2 and n3) each with an output voltage (U2 and U3).
